threat_model.system_context: Agentic/assistant-style web research and tool-mediated actions (e.g., browsing, summarization, optional external app/tool hooks).

primary_assets.why_it_matters: Defines allowed scope; drift enables prompt-injection success.
Improper execution can cause irreversible side effects.

primary_assets.asset: Sensitive data in context (PII, credentials, tokens)
primary_assets.why_it_matters: Exfiltration risk via web content, logs, or citations.

actors.capabilities: Controls page text/HTML; can embed adversarial instructions.
Compromises a reputable site, dependency, or CDN to deliver injected content.
actors.notes: Harder to detect via domain reputation alone.

actors.capabilities: Requests excessive scopes; can leak data or execute actions.

actors.notes: Frequently triggers safety incidents via misunderstanding rather than malice.

trust_boundaries.boundary: Model core vs. retrieved web content
trust_boundaries.risk: Treating retrieved text as instruction rather than untrusted data.
Tool execution bypasses “just text” safety if not gated.

attack_surfaces.examples: Hidden instructions, roleplay hijacks, data-exfil prompts.
Using citations to ‘legitimize’ untrusted or irrelevant claims.
Placeholders, ellipses, missing fields, or non-persisted artifacts.
Actions executed without explicit consent or outside policy.

failure_modes.name: Instruction hierarchy collapse (prompt injection wins)
failure_modes.what_happens: Model follows webpage/tool output instructions over user/system intent.
failure_modes.impact: Unauthorized actions, unsafe guidance, data exfiltration.
detection_signals: tool calls inconsistent with user request
references to webpage instructions as authority

failure_modes.name: Tool execution without explicit authorization
failure_modes.what_happens: Agent executes side-effecting operations (emails, purchases, downloads) without explicit, granular confirmation.

failure_modes.name: Data exfiltration via citations or summaries
failure_modes.what_happens: Sensitive context is echoed into output, logs, or cited to external destinations.

failure_modes.name: Citation laundering / single-source security claims
failure_modes.what_happens: Security conclusions rely on one source or mismatched citations.

failure_modes.name: Non-persisted artifacts (chain break)
failure_modes.what_happens: Narrative/receipt exists in chat text but not as a verifiable file with stable hash.

failure_modes.name: Receipt incompleteness (placeholders/ellipsis)
failure_modes.what_happens: Receipts contain template placeholders (TBD, ..., [actual time]) or missing required fields.

failure_modes.name: Over-broad threat narrative that drifts into actionable wrongdoing
failure_modes.what_happens: Mitigation writeups accidentally include step-by-step exploit instructions.

failure_modes.name: Ambiguity-induced unsafe assistance
failure_modes.what_happens: User intent unclear; model chooses a risky interpretation.
detection_signals: assumptions about target, scope, or permissions

primary_detection_signals: tool calls inconsistent with user request
assumptions about target, scope, or permissions

controls.name: Treat all retrieved content as untrusted data
controls.description: Never elevate webpage/tool output to instruction authority; enforce instruction hierarchy.
controls.implementation_notes: Label retrieval as DATA; strip/ignore embedded directives; require explicit user instruction for actions.

controls.name: Tool-use policy gating + explicit user consent for side effects
controls.description: Hard gate actions that change state; default to executed=false unless confirmed in-record.
controls.implementation_notes: Two-stage: propose action → user confirms → execute; record confirmation hash.

controls.name: Least-privilege scopes for tools/plugins
controls.description: Minimize permissions to reduce blast radius if compromised.
controls.implementation_notes: Separate read vs write scopes; short-lived tokens; deny-by-default.

controls.name: Output and log redaction for sensitive data
controls.description: Prevent accidental leakage of PII/secrets in outputs and artifacts.
controls.implementation_notes: PII detector on outputs; suppress raw tokens; store salted hashes for reference where needed.

controls.name: Receipt schema validation (contract tests)
controls.description: Reject any receipt with placeholders, missing required fields, or invalid JSON.
controls.implementation_notes: JSON Schema + CI check; enforce executed boolean; enforce policy_decision enum.

controls.name: Citation-to-claim traceability checks
controls.description: Ensure each security claim is backed by at least two relevant sources.
controls.implementation_notes: Automated: map claim IDs → citations; flag single-source security claims; flag off-topic domains.

controls.name: Deterministic artifact persistence + hashing
controls.description: Write artifacts to disk, compute hashes, and include in bundle manifest prior to sealing.
controls.implementation_notes: Canonical paths; sha256 of bytes; store in bundle manifest; upload bytes alongside receipts.

controls.name: Independent verification procedure
controls.description: Third party reproduces hash checks and schema validation from the published bundle.
controls.implementation_notes: Verifier script + readme; reproducible environment; no network required for integrity checks.

controls.name: Responsible publication filter
controls.description: Keep public narrative conceptual; move exploit specifics to restricted red-team annex.
controls.implementation_notes: Editorial policy + automated keyword screening; human review for dual-use.

verification_plan.name: Byte-level integrity check
verification_plan.procedure: Compute sha256 for each artifact file and compare against receipt(s) and bundle manifest.

verification_plan.name: Schema validation
verification_plan.procedure: Validate each JSON artifact against the protocol’s required fields; reject placeholders/ellipsis.

verification_plan.name: Citation traceability audit
verification_plan.procedure: For every major security claim, confirm 2+ citations and that citations are relevant to the claim text.

verification_plan.name: Policy decision coherence
verification_plan.procedure: Check that allowed vs refused actions are clearly separated; executed=false for refused; rationale present.

verification_plan.name: Chain linkage verification
verification_plan.procedure: Confirm RUN-007 receipts bind RUN-006 bytes; confirm subsequent RUNs reference prior sha256 values.

verification_plan.name: Independent verifier run
verification_plan.procedure: Run verifier script in clean environment; reproduce all checks without modifying artifacts.

high_confidence_findings.finding: Prompt injection is a primary threat for web-connected LLM systems; mitigations require treating retrieved content as untrusted.
Chain-of-custody requires byte-level persistence + hashes; without bytes, claims cannot be sealed.

medium_confidence_findings.finding: Two-step user confirmations materially reduce tool misuse in agentic settings, but UX tradeoffs can cause bypass pressure.
Citation laundering is a recurring failure mode; automated citation audits reduce risk but require careful tuning.

low_confidence_or_open_questions.question: Exact behavior of ‘Comet’/Perplexity agent modes under adversarial web content (requires fresh empirical runs with raw receipts).
True coverage of regulatory mappings (SOC2/HIPAA/EU AI Act/NIST) for this protocol depends on implementation scope and audits.

research_gaps: RUN-008 raw agent-demo receipt bytes are not present in the workspace; cannot independently validate or seal RUN-008.
RUN-009 was previously referenced but not persisted; this artifact is newly generated and must be hashed and bundled before sealing.
Empirical validation against the live target system (Perplexity Comet/Assistant) should be repeated over time to detect temporal drift.
If publishing externally, obtain legal review for any explicit regulatory claims; keep mappings as ‘alignment hypotheses’ unless audited.

responsible_practice_attestation.dual_use_handling: This narrative stays at a defensive, conceptual level and avoids operational exploit instructions.
responsible_practice_attestation.data_handling: No user secrets or PII are embedded; only source IDs and high-level descriptions are included.
compliance_alignment: EU AI Act mapping is conceptual and requires legal review for deployment specifics.
